courses.title: Principles of Programming
courses.description: Introduces the fundamental concepts of computer programming in Java. Topics include variables, data types, control structures, methods, arrays, and basic OOP.

courses.title: Object-Oriented Programming and Data Structures
courses.description: Object-oriented programming, data structures (linked lists, stacks, queues, trees), generics, interfaces, and exception handling in Java.

courses.title: Programming for Computer Science
courses.description: Systems programming in C/C++. Pointers, memory management, file I/O, Unix/Linux environment, makefiles, debugging tools.

courses.title: Computer Organization and Assembly Language Programming
courses.description: Computer organization, digital logic, assembly language programming, instruction sets, memory systems, and I/O.

courses.title: Introduction to Programming Languages
courses.description: Survey of programming paradigms: imperative, functional, logic, and object-oriented. Languages covered include C, C++, Prolog, Lisp/Scheme.

courses.title: Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.description: Advanced data structures (heaps, hash tables, graphs, balanced trees), algorithm design and analysis, sorting, searching, graph algorithms.

courses.title: Operating Systems
courses.description: Process management, memory management, file systems, I/O, concurrency, synchronization, deadlocks.

courses.title: Principles of Programming Languages
courses.description: Formal language theory, parsing, type systems, semantic analysis, runtime environments.

courses.title: Introduction to Theoretical Computer Science
courses.description: Finite automata, regular languages, context-free grammars, Turing machines, computability, complexity theory (P vs NP).

courses.title: Introduction to Software Engineering
courses.description: Software development lifecycle, requirements, design patterns, testing, agile methodologies, version control, team projects.

courses.title: Information Assurance
courses.description: Computer security fundamentals, cryptography, authentication, network security, vulnerability analysis, security policy.

courses.title: Database Management
courses.description: Relational model, SQL, database design, normalization, transactions, indexing, query optimization.

courses.title: Computer Networks
courses.description: Network architecture, TCP/IP, routing, switching, network security, wireless networks.

courses.title: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
courses.description: Advanced algorithm design techniques: divide and conquer, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, NP-completeness.

courses.title: Software Analysis and Design
courses.description: Advanced software engineering: architecture, design patterns, UML, system modeling, software quality.

courses.title: Computer Network Management
courses.description: Network management protocols, SNMP, network monitoring, configuration management, fault management.

courses.title: Computer Graphics
courses.description: Graphics pipeline, transformations, rendering, shading, texture mapping, OpenGL/WebGL.

courses.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
courses.description: Search algorithms, game playing, knowledge representation, machine learning basics, neural networks, NLP fundamentals.

courses.title: Foundations of Machine Learning
courses.description: Supervised and unsupervised learning, regression, classification, clustering, neural networks, model evaluation.

courses.title: Natural Language Processing
courses.description: Text processing, language models, information extraction, sentiment analysis, machine translation.

courses.title: Introduction to Computer Vision
courses.description: Image formation, feature extraction, object detection, image segmentation, deep learning for vision.

courses.title: Introduction to Robotics
courses.description: Robot kinematics, dynamics, sensors, control, path planning, localization, mapping.

courses.title: Capstone Project I
courses.description: First semester of senior capstone. Team formation, project proposal, requirements analysis, design phase.

courses.title: Capstone Project II
courses.description: Second semester of senior capstone. Implementation, testing, deployment, final presentation.

courses.title: Discrete Mathematical Structures
courses.description: Logic, sets, relations, functions, combinatorics, graph theory, proof techniques.

courses.title: Calculus for Engineers I
courses.description: Limits, derivatives, applications of differentiation, introduction to integration.

courses.title: Calculus for Engineers II
courses.description: Integration techniques, applications of integration, sequences and series.

courses.title: Calculus for Engineers III
courses.description: Multivariable calculus: partial derivatives, multiple integrals, vector calculus.

courses.title: Applied Linear Algebra
courses.description: Matrices, vector spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues, applications.

courses.title: Elementary Statistics
courses.description: Descriptive statistics, probability, sampling distributions, hypothesis testing, regression.

courses.title: University Physics I: Mechanics
courses.description: Newtonian mechanics, kinematics, dynamics, energy, momentum, rotational motion.

courses.title: University Physics Lab I
courses.description: Laboratory experiments in mechanics.

courses.title: University Physics II: Electricity and Magnetism
courses.description: Electric fields, magnetic fields, circuits, electromagnetic waves, optics.

courses.title: University Physics Lab II
courses.description: Laboratory experiments in electricity, magnetism, and optics.

courses.title: First-Year Composition
courses.description: Academic writing, rhetorical analysis, research skills, argumentation.

courses.title: First-Year Composition
courses.description: Research-based writing, multimodal composition, critical analysis.

courses.title: Technical Writing
courses.description: Writing for technical and professional audiences. Reports, proposals, documentation.

courses.title: Introduction to Philosophy
courses.description: Survey of major philosophical questions and methods.

courses.title: Introduction to Psychology
courses.description: Survey of psychology: behavior, cognition, development, social psychology, abnormal psychology.

courses.title: Macroeconomic Principles
courses.description: National income, unemployment, inflation, monetary and fiscal policy.

courses.title: Peoples and Cultures of the World
courses.description: Introduction to cultural anthropology: cultural diversity, globalization, identity.

courses.title: World Regional Geography
courses.description: Physical and human geography of world regions.

courses.title: Western Civilization 1600 to Present
courses.description: European and global history from 1600 to the present.

courses.title: Small Group Communication
courses.description: Theory and practice of communication in small groups.
